facts_disclosed: Lives with Dev in Easton, £475 each

turns.content: do u know anything about mould in houses? like how bad is it actually for ur health

Mould can actually be quite serious for your health, depending on the type and how much you're exposed to. Here's the breakdown:

**Short-term exposure:**
- Irritated eyes, nose, and throat
- Coughing, sneezing, congestion
- Skin irritation
- Worsened asthma or allergies

**Long-term exposure:**
- Chronic respiratory issues
- Increased risk of developing asthma
- Persistent fatigue and headaches
- In rare cases, some moulds (like black mould / Stachybotrys) can produce mycotoxins that cause more serious health effects

**Who's most at risk:**
- People with asthma or allergies
- People with weakened immune systems
- Children and elderly people

The biggest factor is how much and how long. A small patch on a bathroom ceiling is different from mould spreading across a bedroom wall.

Are you dealing with a mould situation? I can give more specific advice depending on where it is and how bad it's got.

yeah theres some in the bathroom. our bathroom dont have a window init so the ventilation is peak. me and my housemate been going back and forth about whether to tell the landlord or just deal with it ourselves

A bathroom with no window is a recipe for mould — it's a really common issue. The moisture from showers has nowhere to go, so it just sits on the walls and ceiling.

On the landlord question — I'd say **tell the landlord**, and here's why:

**Your rights as a tenant (in the UK):**
- Under the Homes (Fitness for Human Habitation) Act 2018, your landlord has a legal obligation to keep the property free from serious hazards, including damp and mould
- Mould caused by a structural issue (like no ventilation) is the landlord's responsibility, not yours
- If you fix it yourself and it comes back (which it will without proper ventilation), you'll just keep spending money on a problem that isn't yours to fix

**What to ask the landlord for:**
- An **extractor fan** installed in the bathroom — this is the real fix. Without it, any cleaning is just temporary
- A proper assessment of whether there's damp behind the walls

**In the meantime, to manage it:**
- Clean the visible mould with a mix of white vinegar or a bleach solution (1 part bleach to 10 parts water)
- Leave the bathroom door open after showers to let steam escape into the rest of the house
- If you have a dehumidifier, run it nearby

Is this a rented place? How long have you been living there?

yeah rented. I live with my boy Dev in Easton, we pay 475 each so its not bad for Bristol tbh. hes a Deliveroo rider so the house constantly smells like food which is calm but the bathroom situation is annoying

£475 each in Easton is genuinely decent for Bristol, especially right now — rents have gone mad across the city. So I get why you wouldn't want to rock the boat with the landlord.

But here's the thing — reporting mould isn't rocking the boat. It's a legitimate maintenance issue, and a reasonable landlord will sort it because:
1. It protects their property from long-term damage
2. They're legally obligated to
3. An extractor fan is a relatively cheap fix (£100-200 installed)

**How to approach it:**
Send a polite email or text (so you have it in writing) saying something like:

*"Hi [landlord], we've noticed mould developing in the bathroom. As there's no window or extractor fan, we think ventilation might be the issue. Could you arrange for someone to have a look? Happy to discuss. Thanks, Marcus and Dev."*

Keeping it friendly and in writing means there's a record if it ever escalates — but honestly, most landlords will just sort it.

**While you wait:**
- Keep the bathroom door open after every shower
- Wipe down the walls after showering (annoying but effective)
- A cheap dehumidifier from Argos (around £30-40) can help pull moisture out

Do you and Dev both use the same bathroom? If you're both showering daily in a small space with zero ventilation, that's a lot of moisture with nowhere to go.

yeah its the only bathroom lol. and Dev showers like 3 times a day coz hes cycling around delivering food all day so hes always sweaty 😂 ill send the landlord a message tho good idea doing it in writing

Three showers a day in a bathroom with no ventilation — yeah that's your problem right there! That's a huge amount of steam building up with absolutely nowhere to go. No wonder the mould is having a field day.

Doing it in writing is always the smart move. If the landlord is decent, they'll just arrange a fix and that's that. If they're slow or unresponsive, that written record becomes important.

**Quick timeline to keep in mind:**
- Send the message this week
- Give them about 14 days to respond and arrange something
- If nothing happens, send a follow-up referencing the original message
- If they still ignore it, you can contact Bristol City Council's environmental health team — they take damp and mould complaints seriously and can force landlords to act

But hopefully it won't come to that. Most landlords would rather spend £150 on a fan than deal with a council inspection.

**One more tip for Dev specifically:** If he can open the bathroom door straight after showering and even crack a window somewhere else in the house to create airflow, that'll make a noticeable difference immediately. Cross-ventilation is the key when the room itself has no window.

Good luck with the message — and tell Dev to keep that Deliveroo money coming so you can keep that rent going!
haha trust me the deliveroo hustle is real. safe for the advice tho ill text the landlord tonight. the mould aint that bad yet but better to sort it now before it gets proper grim

Exactly — catching it early is the move. Small patch now is an easy fix. Leave it six months and you're looking at mould spreading behind tiles, potential damp damage to the walls, and a much bigger (and more expensive) job.

Good luck with the landlord. And remember — if you clean the visible mould this weekend with a vinegar or bleach solution, the bathroom will look much better immediately. Just make sure to wear a mask and gloves when you're scrubbing, and keep airflow going.

Hope you and Dev get it sorted quickly. Enjoy the free Deliveroo smells in the meantime!
